name: Bexhill
content: Bexhill is a coastal town in southern England with a generally helpful populace. I disembarked a train here to make a bus connection on my way to Seven Sisters national park. Now, for the life of me, I cannot remember why I disembarked at Bexhill. The train runs all the way to Eastbourne, which is much closer to Seven Sisters than Bexhill. But I digress. Unbeknownst to me, the bus stop I was looking for had temporarily been moved. After waiting for half an hour in a residential neighborhood for the bus, an older Indian gentleman started telling me directions to the new bus stop location, much to the protest of his family who was out on a walk with him. Maybe this grandfather giving directions to disoriented travelers is such a common occurrence that his family has come to loathe it? Either way, his directions very much saved me that day, so thank you!
